selections.name: data-model
selections.registry_description: Surfaces the entities, attributes, and relationships implied by the requirements so forms, lists, and detail views can be designed against an explicit schema.

selections.name: jtbd
selections.registry_description: Surfaces the jobs and outcomes users hire the product for so design decisions anchor to user motivation rather than features.

selections.name: ooux
selections.registry_description: Identifies the core objects, attributes, and CTAs implied by the requirements so screens and navigation can be structured around them.

selections.name: use-cases
selections.registry_description: Surfaces actor goals, preconditions, and main/extension flows implied by the requirements so screens and APIs are designed against explicit usage paths.

selections.name: user-journeys
selections.registry_description: Maps the experience phases, pain-points, and opportunities the requirements describe so feature prioritisation targets the moments that matter most.

selections.name: task-flows
selections.registry_description: Surfaces the goal-decomposition structure and step-by-step user paths implicit in the requirements before screens, wizards, or form sequences are designed.

selections.name: trade-off-dimension-analysis
selections.registry_description: Scores each user goal against a curated set of UX trade-off dimensions (Speed vs Accuracy, Simplicity vs Power, Automation vs Control, ...) to guide wireframing posture per goal.
